mission.title: vitess: vtgate queries randomly routed to unhealthy or non-existent replicas
mission.description: vtgate queries randomly routed to unhealthy or non-existent replicas. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current vitess deployment
steps.description:
Verify your vitess version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
helm list -n vitess 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working vitess installation.

steps.title: Review vitess configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant vitess configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
kubectl get configmap -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=vitess
```
#### Overview of the Issue

queries sent to `` like `SET ..` are sent randomly to alphabetically first keyspace replicas or keyspaces without replicas or keyspaces with all replicas unhealthy.

steps.title: Apply the fix for vtgate queries randomly routed to unhealthy or non-existent
steps.description:
Rather than returning the error, we will just log the error as the intention for executing the query it to validate the current setting and eventually ignore it anyways. There is no benefit of returning the error back to client.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n vitess -l app.kubernetes.io/name=vitess
kubectl get events -n vitess --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "vtgate queries randomly routed to unhealthy or non-existent" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Rather than returning the error, we will just log the error as the intention for executing the query it to validate the current setting and eventually ignore it anyways. There is no benefit of returning the error back to client.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with vitess installed or the issue environment reproducible.
